title: Move noCache logic lower in stack
body:
Fixes #4111

One or more tests are not taking advantage of the local image cache.
This has been observed to cause a testing flake in at least one
`--sigproxy` test which uses `PodmanTestIntegration.PodmanPID()`.
It has a rather short timeout of 15-seconds, which isn't always
enough time to pull down a remote image.

Fix this by reloacing the `noCache` logic from
`PodmanTest.PodmanAsUserBase()` down the stack into
`PodmanTestIntegration.makeOptions()`.  This also eliminates the need to
also check if a remote-client is being used - since it uses a different
function.

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

comments.body: Looks like you checked in `goecho` - that should probably be gitignored, but it definitely shouldn't be committed
@mheon okay, should be fixed now.  I'm a little worried at the inventiveness of the changes here, let's see what CI says...
...hmm, welp, my argument processing changes seem to work well, [but there's still a flake](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6302212726194176) with `bash: /h/fifo: No such file or directory` problem.
Cherry-pick of those fixes from Matt's PR should take care of the fifo thing, lessee...
force-push: removed cherry-pick and "go faster" instrumentation.
Okay, I think this is ready.
